rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN T. WEST et al VS. LAWRENCE EBRINGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Adn Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Withheld On Attorneys-Client Priviledge Grounds, Or In The Alternative, Request For An In Camera Review Or Appointment O Fdiscovery Referee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 31, 2014, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT LAWRENCE EBRINGER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Withheld On Attorneys-Client Privilege Grounds, Or In The Alternative, Request For An In Camera Review Or Appointment Of Discovery Referee Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. As a preliminary finding the Court finds based on the declarations of Stephen West and Aimee West that Ron Albert was an attorney retained by them which sets the stage for their claim of privilege. See Alpha Beta Co. vs. Superior Court (1984) 157 CA3rd 818, 824-825. The burden has thereupon shifted to the moving party to disprove the fact said relationship existed. The Wests have declined to stipulate to an in-camera review of the purportedly privileged documents as is their statutory right. [Evidence Code section 915(a]). However, the Court has the power to order an Evidence Code section 402 ¿preliminary fact¿ hearing, including taking live testimony, on whether the privilege exists. Therefore the Court requests that one or both of the parties claiming the privilege appear at the hearing for that purpose, at which time it will be finally determined if a 402 hearing is needed. At the outset the moving party is advised the Court is not convinced the ¿dominant purpose¿ doctrine in the context of the facts of this action ¿ the realtor/broker is also an attorney - precludes assertion of the attorney-client privilege for those communications undertaken in the attorney context. Oral argument will be directed to that issue. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deft'S Subpoenas For Medical And Employment Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 31, 2014, line 1, PLAINTIFF JANE ROE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendant's Subpoenas For Medical And Employment Records Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash with respect to plaintiff¿s previous employment/employer records is denied on the ground the Court on April 3, 2013 ruled that the precise topics covered by the instant subpoena are relevant and time-frame appropriate. That Court in fact modified the scope of the subpoenas under discussion which modifications have been adopted for the subject subpoenas. The question for this Court is solely whether records from plaintiff¿s previous employment/employer are relevant and/or likely to lead to the admissibility of relevant evidence. The finding is that burden has been met. With respect to the medical records subpoena, the motion to quash is also denied. However, plaintiff¿s counsel is given a ¿first look¿ opportunity to determine if any documents not within the scope of the subpoena have been included by the health care provider. All subpoenaed documents produced through the medical record deposition subpoena shall be delivered directly to the plaintiff ¿s attorney. The plaintiff ¿s attorney shall then have 10 days to review them and produce them to defense counsel along with a detailed log of anything removed or redacted. If defense counsel disputes an entry in the privilege log the attorneys shall ¿meet and confer¿. If the parties cannot agree the Court will undertake an in camera review of the disputed records. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested or supply same in digital format to the Judge Pro Tem. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS A. WARD VS. FLIESLER MEYER LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 31, 2014, Line 11: CROSS DEFENDANT THOMAS WARD'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled. Cross-Complainant Fliesler Meyer LLP has pled sufficient facts to allege each cause of action. Each cause of action is not clearly and affirmatively barred by the statute of limitations based on the alleged period of misconduct from 2005 to 2012. For the sixth cause of action, Cross-Complainant alleges conversion of firm property and money. (Cross-Complaint, 70.) A general demurrer does not lie to only part of a cause of action. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Applications For Right To Attach Orders Against Larry R. Deatherage As Trustee Of The Kenneth Rawlings Trust Of 2002
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 31, 2014, Line 16: PLAINTIFF STEAD FINANCIAL, INC.'S, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, Applications For Right To Attach Orders Against Larry R. Deatherage As Trustee Of The Kenneth Rawlings Trust Of 2002 Granted as to the Application for Right to Attach Order against Defendants Clark Wallace, individually, and Clark Wallace and Geraldine Wallace, as Trustees of the Clark E. Wallace and Geraldine M. Wallace Trust dated July 9, 1991 pursuant to the stipulation of the parties. Denied as to the Wallace Defendants' Claim of Exemption pertaining to their residence under CCP § 704.115. The Wallace Defendants' residence does not qualify as part of a Private Retirement Plan. (CCP § 704.115; In re Barnes (Bankr. E.D. Cal. 2002) 275 B.R. 889, 897 - 898.) rulings.Case Title: EVAN C NELSON VS. TUCKER ELLIS, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Arb And To Stay State Court Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 31, 2014, Line 17: DEFENDANT TUCKER ELLIS, LLP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay State Court Proceedings The Court denies the motion to compel arbitration. The agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it was presented to plaintiff in a take-it-or-leave-it manner and defendant failed to provide a copy of the rules that govern the arbitration to plaintiff. The court further notes that the parties' agreement treats plaintiff as an employee. The agreement is substantively unconscionable and the requirements of Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychare Services, Inc., (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, apply to this case. Boghos v. Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's of London (2005) 36 Cal.4th 495, does not control because it was not an employment case and the instant plaintiff's claim for invasion of privacy is tethered to the California Constitution's right to privacy. The parties' agreement does not comply with Armendariz because: (1) Section 17.3 of the CPR rules allows the arbitrator to shift all fees/costs to plaintiff; (2) Paragraph 9.6(c)(v) [no formal discovery] is cryptic and it is unclear exactly what type of discovery plaintiff is entitled to. That clause ought to be construed against defendant drafter; and (3) the agreement impermissibly bars the recovery of punitive damages. The Court determines that severance is not appropriate given the number of unconscionable provisions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL PHARMACY ALLIANCE A CORPORATION VS. TOBY DOUGLAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, January 31, 2014, Line 18: Petitioner's Motion For Preliminary Injunction Denied. Petitioners have not shown a likelihood of success on the merits for their petition for writ of mandate because Petitioners have not shown that Respondents failed to perform a ministerial duty. (See Venice Town Council, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 113, 138.) Welfare & Institutions Code § 10000 does not create a mandatory ministerial duty for which Respondents are required to perform. Even if Petitioners could show a likelihood of success, they cannot establish irreparable injury. The evidence shows that Respondents have exempted a large quantity of drug products from the 10% provider payment reductions, which will reduce the financial impact to Petitioners. (See Declaration of Hendrix in Support of Opposition, 12-17.) Further, Petitioners have not shown that they cannot be fully compensated by the payment of damages for their alleged injury. Balancing the equities, there are public policy considerations against enjoining public agencies from performing their duties. (See Tahoe Keys Property Owners' Assn. v. State Water Resources Control Bd. (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1459, 1471.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
